2020-12-01.title: Bank Robbers in Brazil Terrorize a Sleepy City, Overpowering Police
2020-12-01.abstract: An organized gang struck the southern city of Criciúma with guns and explosives, robbing a bank and briefly taking hostages. Hours later, Brazilians reported another gang robbery over 2,000 miles away.

2020-12-01.title: New ‘BTS Law’ Is Passed in South Korea. An Army of Fans Rejoices.
2020-12-01.abstract: The oldest member of BTS was saved at the last minute from having to join the military under the country’s conscription system.

2020-12-01.title: France has kept schools open without driving up infections, and other news from around the world.
2020-12-01.abstract: France’s coronavirus test positivity rate, at 11.1 percent, is nearly four times that of New York City.

2020-12-01.title: In Nagorno-Karabakh Peace Deal, Putin Applied a Deft New Touch
2020-12-01.abstract: The iron-fisted tactics used against Georgia and Ukraine seem to have fallen out of favor, replaced by a more subtle blend of soft power and an implicit military threat.

2020-12-01.title: Vaccines are coming, but pandemic experts expect a ‘horrible’ winter.
2020-12-01.abstract: With a new administration and coronavirus vaccines on the way, there is hope for the United States, even as it grapples with the appalling milestone of more than one million new coronavirus cases every week.

2020-12-02.title: China Lands Robotic Spacecraft on the Moon
2020-12-02.abstract: The Chang’e-5 spacecraft landed on Tuesday on the moon, where it will spend two days gathering rocks and soil from the lunar surface. This was the third successful uncrewed moon landing by China since 2013.

2020-12-02.title: Is China Laying Claim to Kimchi, Too? Some South Koreans Think So
2020-12-02.abstract: When a state tabloid suggested that China had set a global standard for kimchi, social media users accused Beijing of misappropriating a Korean culinary staple.

2020-12-02.title: U.K. Approves Pfizer Coronavirus Vaccine, a First in the West
2020-12-02.abstract: The emergency approval, ahead of the United States and the European Union, clears the way for Britain to begin mass inoculations. “Help is on its way,” one official said.

2020-12-02.title: Joshua Wong and Agnes Chow Are Sentenced to Prison Over Hong Kong Protest
2020-12-02.abstract: Activists denounced the prison terms for illegal assembly charges over a 2019 demonstration outside Police Headquarters.

2020-12-02.title: ‘He Is a Thug’: Polarizing Muslim Cleric Returns to Indonesia
2020-12-02.abstract: Rizieq Shihab and his vigilante movement have spent decades calling for harsh Islamic rule. Now he’s back from self-imposed exile and promising a “moral revolution.”

2020-12-03.title: He Escaped Death as a Kamikaze Pilot. 70 Years Later, He Told His Story.
2020-12-03.abstract: Kazuo Odachi is one of the last living members of a group never meant to survive. He wants to remind Japan that before its modern success came the sacrifices of the young pilots who gave their lives.

2020-12-03.title: Jimmy Lai, Hong Kong Media Tycoon, Is Denied Bail on Fraud Charge
2020-12-03.abstract: Mr. Lai, who founded the pro-democracy newspaper Apple Daily, was ordered jailed until April.

2020-12-03.title: U.S. Tightens Visa Rules for Chinese Communist Party Members
2020-12-03.abstract: New guidelines mean that China’s 92 million party members will be limited to one-month, single-entry U.S. permits — if the State Department can figure out who they are.

2020-12-03.title: The College-Entrance Exam Is 9 Hours Long. Covid-19 Made It Harder.
2020-12-03.abstract: In South Korea, planes are grounded and parents pray as high school students hunker down for the grueling test. But this year, officials and students had to navigate a pandemic.

2020-12-03.title: ‘Field of Broken Dreams’: London’s Growing Taxi Graveyards
2020-12-03.abstract: Confronted with deserted streets during the pandemic, drivers are turning in their rented black cabs by the hundreds.

2020-12-04.title: Why Is Australia Still Part of the British Monarchy?
2020-12-04.abstract: Exploring why we just can’t seem to break free.

2020-12-04.title: This Time, a Cartoon Depiction of Naomi Osaka Is More True to Life
2020-12-04.abstract: Last year, the tennis champion was shown with light skin in an instant noodle ad. Now, she’ll be portrayed as a manga character, and illustrators insisted on getting details right.

2020-12-04.title: 2 Hurricanes Devastated Central America. Will the Ruin Spur a Migration Wave?
2020-12-04.abstract: The storms displaced hundreds of thousands of people, creating a new class of refugees with more reason than ever to migrate north and setting up an early test for the incoming Biden administration.

2020-12-04.title: When the second wave is deadlier than the first: How could Europeans allow this to happen?
2020-12-04.abstract: By early June, scarred and battered, Europe was emerging from the depths of its fight against the coronavirus pandemic.

2020-12-04.title: We asked 700 epidemiologists what it would take for their lives to return to normal.
2020-12-04.abstract: Even with coronavirus vaccines on the way, many epidemiologists do not expect their lives to return to pre-pandemic normal until most Americans are vaccinated. In the meantime, most have eased up on some precautions — now going to the grocery store or seeing friends outdoors, for example — but are as cautious as ever about many activities of daily life.

2020-12-05.title: Vaccines End the Pandemic’s Political Harmony
2020-12-05.abstract: Erin O’Toole, the Conservative leader, is using the government’s vaccine plans to begin his first big political attack on Prime Minister Justin Trudeau.

2020-12-05.title: After Perilous Atlantic Journey, Migrants Await Their Fate in Canary Island Hotels
2020-12-05.abstract: The Spanish government has put migrants up in hotels as the islands struggle to cope with a rise in arrivals from Africa, but tensions are fraying with locals who fear they’ll scare off tourists.

2020-12-05.title: Greek Orthodox Church Faces Criticism as Virus Hits Its Ranks
2020-12-05.abstract: Church leaders have backed government restrictions, but defiance by hard-liners and a commitment to the sacrament of communion, taken with a shared spoon, may undercut good intentions.

2020-12-05.title: Mystery Couple Found in a Roll of Film From Nearly 70 Years Ago
2020-12-05.abstract: Internet sleuths have been captivated by the photos, which were recently developed and document a couple’s trip to the Swiss-Italian border with a dachshund.

2020-12-05.title: As Refugees, Their Options Were Limited. Others Saw a Profit.
2020-12-05.abstract: Even after decades in Thailand, Kayan women who fled Myanmar make their living from their traditional neck rings. But that’s changing.

2020-12-06.title: China Peddles Falsehoods to Obscure Origin of Covid Pandemic
2020-12-06.abstract: To push the idea that the virus didn’t come from China, the government has misrepresented experts’ remarks and given dubious theories the veneer of science.

2020-12-06.title: Bergamo’s Pandemic Survivors Carry Scars Unseen and Incalculable
2020-12-06.abstract: Plagued by guilt, anger and regrets, those still alive in the hardest hit place in Italy, and perhaps the world, grapple to understand how the virus has changed them.

2020-12-06.title: China peddles falsehoods to push the idea that the virus came from somewhere else.
2020-12-06.abstract: Facing global anger over their initial mishandling of the outbreak, the Chinese authorities are now trying to rewrite the narrative of the pandemic by pushing theories that the virus originated outside China.

2020-12-06.title: A Polarizing Documentary Spurs Debate Over a Violent Time in Quebec
2020-12-06.abstract: Fifty years after separatist violence prompted the government to send soldiers to Montreal, Quebec is grappling with how to remember the 1970 “October crisis.”

2020-12-06.title: ‘Like a Cattle Yard’: How Justice Is Delivered in Australia’s Bush Courts
2020-12-06.abstract: In some remote Aboriginal communities, judges and lawyers arrive by plane and handle dozens of cases in a single day. Critics say language barriers and the rapid pace infringe on human rights.

2020-12-07.title: Australia Gears Up for the Great Koala Count, Using Drones, Droppings and Dogs
2020-12-07.abstract: The marsupials are not easy to find, or count accurately, so officials will deploy new methods.

2020-12-07.title: An Autistic Man Is Killed, Exposing Israel’s Festering Police Brutality Problem
2020-12-07.abstract: The police killing of a disabled Palestinian fueled nationwide protests. But the authorities have failed to rein in the use of excessive force, which has a long history.

2020-12-07.title: The Best Movies and TV Shows New to Netflix, Amazon and Stan in Australia in December
2020-12-07.abstract: Our streaming picks for December, including ‘The Prom,’ ‘Ma Rainey’s Black Bottom,’ and ‘Your Honor.’

2020-12-07.title: A Bronx neighborhood reels with 26 percent unemployment.
2020-12-07.abstract: More than one in four workers in the West Farms neighborhood of the Bronx are out of work.

2020-12-07.title: Police in Indonesia Kill 6 Followers of Hard-Line Cleric
2020-12-07.abstract: The authorities said the men, adherents of the Muslim cleric Rizieq Shihab, were shot and killed after they attacked officers in an unmarked car that had been following them.

2020-12-08.title: Christchurch Inquiry Says New Zealand Couldn’t Have Prevented Mosque Attacks
2020-12-08.abstract: But the Royal Commission, the country’s highest-level investigation, faulted lax gun regulations and the country’s “fragile” intelligence agencies.

2020-12-08.title: Israeli Soccer Team, Infamous for Anti-Arab Fans, Has New Co-Owner: a Sheikh
2020-12-08.abstract: The barrier-shattering deal puts an Emirati royal at the helm of Beitar Jerusalem, the only Israeli team that has never fielded an Arab player and whose most extreme fans chant racist slurs.

2020-12-08.title: As Britain begins vaccinations, here are answers to some common questions.
2020-12-08.abstract: Britain’s National Health Service began delivering shots of the Pfizer-BioNTech vaccine on Tuesday, opening a public health campaign with little precedent in modern medicine.

2020-12-08.title: The U.S. has recorded its most deaths in a week.
2020-12-08.abstract: The United States has recorded its most coronavirus-related deaths over a weeklong period, as a brutal surge gathers speed across the country.

2020-12-08.title: First vaccine recipients hope to set an example and are looking forward to holidays with family.
2020-12-08.abstract: On Tuesday, a handful of people across Britain — mostly those 80 and over, health care workers and those working in nursing homes — began receiving the newly approved Pfizer vaccine. It was the first day that the inoculations were being administered in any Western nation.

2020-12-09.title: A Year Like No Other: 2020 in Pictures
2020-12-09.abstract: In a world where life changed seemingly overnight, photographers transformed how they worked, trading intimacy for distance. This is what they captured.

2020-12-09.title: He Broke Out of Quarantine for 8 Seconds, and Got a $3,550 Fine
2020-12-09.abstract: Around the world, flouting coronavirus regulations can have expensive consequences.

2020-12-09.title: In Russia, vaccines are free. But public distrust is high.
2020-12-09.abstract: Russia has made its coronavirus vaccine available for free in recent days to teachers, medical workers and social-service employees younger than 61 in Moscow. But a lack of trust is hobbling Russia’s rollout: the country’s scientists may well have made great strides in battling the pandemic, but many Russians are not ready to believe it.

2020-12-09.title: Children’s hospitals are pitching in to help with the flood of adult Covid-19 patients.
2020-12-09.abstract: With a rising tide of Covid-19 patients threatening to overwhelm hospitals, public health officials across the United States are reaching for a safety valve that the Northeast used in the spring: borrowing beds in children’s hospitals to care for adults.

2020-12-09.title: She Accused the Mayor of Sexual Assault. Then the Town Turned on Her.
2020-12-09.abstract: Assemblymen in Kusatsu, Japan, organized an election to recall their sole female colleague, saying she had damaged the town’s reputation with her allegations.

2020-12-10.title: The virus spreads at wildfire speed in tiny Rhode Island, surpassing rates of new cases elsewhere.
2020-12-10.abstract: Rhode Island, the smallest U.S. state in area, now has the fastest spread of coronavirus, with more new cases per capita being reported than any other state, according to a New York Times database.

2020-12-10.title: It’s Australia’s First Big Blaze of the Fire Season. How Bad Will the Summer Get?
2020-12-10.abstract: Last year, the country suffered catastrophic wildfires. Now, it is watching as a scenic getaway burns. What the rest of the season brings may depend on heat waves, winds and dried-out grass.

2020-12-10.title: Cases Surge in South Korea, but Covid Vaccine Is Months Away
2020-12-10.abstract: Health officials warned that the number of daily cases in the country could rise to record highs in coming days, and that the latest wave of infections was the most elusive.

2020-12-10.title: South Korea faces its ‘biggest ever coronavirus crisis’ and other news around the world.
2020-12-10.abstract: For most of the year, South Korea has kept its coronavirus numbers so low it was the envy of the world. Now, the country is grappling with the most elusive wave of infections it has seen, just as other nations prepare to roll out vaccinations.

2020-12-10.title: An Unwelcome Silent Night: Germany Without Christmas Markets
2020-12-10.abstract: Across the country, city and town squares stand empty of the usual huts, sounds, scents and lights, as the coronavirus has forced the country to skip its beloved annual Christmas markets.

2020-12-11.title: After more than 3,000 U.S. deaths in a day, a warning: ‘The worst is yet to come.’
2020-12-11.abstract: The new daily record of coronavirus deaths is a stunning number in and of itself.

2020-12-11.title: Israel-Morocco Deal Follows History of Cooperation on Arms and Spying
2020-12-11.abstract: For almost 60 years, the two countries, which agreed to normalize ties, have worked together closely but secretly on military and intelligence matters, assassinations, and migration of Jews to Israel.

2020-12-11.title: The Women Faced Off to Play Soccer. One Team Lost Because of Hair Dye.
2020-12-11.abstract: A Chinese university team argued that an opposing player’s hair was not “black enough,” according to the rules, and her team forfeited the match.

2020-12-11.title: Reporting on Indigenous Australia
2020-12-11.abstract: The more difficult the access to a story, the more often it is worth doing.

2020-12-11.title: Jimmy Lai, Media Mogul, Is Charged Under Hong Kong’s Security Law
2020-12-11.abstract: Mr. Lai’s case was followed hours later by a disclosure that the authorities in China had detained a Chinese citizen working for Bloomberg News.

2020-12-12.title: U.K. to Halt Subsidies for Fossil Fuel Projects Abroad
2020-12-12.abstract: Prime Minister Boris Johnson has been positioning himself as a leader in fighting global warming, an area where he can make common cause with President-elect Joseph R. Biden Jr.

2020-12-12.title: A Vaccine Is on Its Way to Canada. Who Will Get It First?
2020-12-12.abstract: Determining how to parcel out the long-awaited vaccine is not a simple task for the provinces.

2020-12-12.title: Protests Erupt as South Korea’s Most Notorious Rapist Walks Free
2020-12-12.abstract: Cho Doo-soon served 12 years in prison for raping an 8-year-old girl. His case has led the national legislature to create new rules for sex offenders released from prison.

2020-12-12.title: Bhutan Becomes Latest Asian Nation to Dial Back Anti-Gay Laws
2020-12-12.abstract: Lawmakers in the Himalayan country voted this week to amend a 2004 law that criminalizes “sexual conduct that is against the order of nature,” previously treated as a reference to gay sex.

2020-12-12.title: As France Shifts Right, a Hard-Line Minister Proves Indispensable to Macron
2020-12-12.abstract: Interior Minister Gérald Darmanin has become a useful tool for the French president as he responds to growing calls for law and order following a spate of terror attacks.

2020-12-13.title: She Stalked Her Daughter’s Killers Across Mexico, One by One
2020-12-13.abstract: Armed with a handgun, a fake ID card and disguises, Miriam Rodríguez was a one-woman detective squad, defying a system where criminal impunity often prevails.

2020-12-13.title: Vaccine distribution is about to begin in the virus-ravaged U.S.
2020-12-13.abstract: The first of nearly three million doses of the first Covid-19 vaccine were packed in dry ice and put on trucks at a Pfizer plant in Kalamazoo, Mich., on Sunday morning, destined for hundreds of distribution centers in all 50 states, the most ambitious vaccination campaign in American history.

2020-12-13.title: Going ‘the Extra Mile,’ Britain and E.U. Give Brexit Talks More Time
2020-12-13.abstract: The two sides agreed to extend a deadline on the seemingly intractable trade negotiations in an attempt to avoid a disruptive no-deal outcome before Dec. 31.

2020-12-13.title: Bahrain is the second country to approve a Chinese vaccine, and other news around the world.
2020-12-13.abstract: Bahrain said on Sunday that it had approved the use of a Chinese vaccine against the coronavirus, after the United Arab Emirates became the first government to do so on Wednesday.

2020-12-13.title: Mumbai Police Arrest TV Chief as Media Dispute in India Intensifies
2020-12-13.abstract: Vikas Khanchandani was accused of involvement in an effort to falsely bolster ratings data at Republic TV, a right-wing news broadcaster. The network said the allegations were politically motivated.

2020-12-14.title: China’s Combative Nationalists See a World Turning Their Way
2020-12-14.abstract: China’s Communist Party is pushing the narrative that the pandemic has proved the superiority of its authoritarian model. The muscular message is finding fans at home.

2020-12-14.title: U.K. to Ease Rules on Blood Donations by Gay and Bisexual Men
2020-12-14.abstract: The new policy, which will take effect next summer, was described by Britain’s health secretary as a landmark and by an activist as “a fundamental shift toward recognizing people are individuals.”

2020-12-14.title: Trump delays a plan to fast-track vaccines for White House staff members.
2020-12-14.abstract: President Trump said on Sunday night that he would delay a plan for senior White House staff members to receive the coronavirus vaccine in the coming days.

2020-12-14.title: A new study shows an arthritis drug may help Covid-19 patients recover.
2020-12-14.abstract: Adding an arthritis drug called baricitinib to Covid-19 treatment regimens that include the antiviral drug remdesivir might shave a day or more off recovery times, especially for those who are seriously sick, according to a study published on Friday.

2020-12-14.title: Sudan Celebrates ‘New Era’ as U.S. Drops It From Terrorism List
2020-12-14.abstract: The Trump administration formally removed the country from a list of state sponsors of terrorism on Monday, ending nearly three decades of international isolation.

2020-12-15.title: Turkey’s Coffeehouses, a Hub of Male Social Life, May Not Survive Virus
2020-12-15.abstract: Coffeehouses, mainstays of Turkish neighborhoods for centuries, are suffering under pandemic restrictions — particularly a ban on games. Regulars fear losing “our jokes, our laughter.”

2020-12-15.title: Putin Congratulates Biden After Electoral College Vote
2020-12-15.abstract: The Russian leader had delayed acknowledging Mr. Biden’s victory for weeks, citing President Trump’s legal challenges. The leaders of Mexico and Brazil also offered congratulations for the first time.

2020-12-15.title: Delivery Workers in South Korea Say They’re Dying of ‘Overwork’
2020-12-15.abstract: More than a dozen couriers have died this year. Some died after complaining of unbearable workloads that kept them on the clock from dawn until past midnight.

2020-12-15.title: Rich countries have first dibs on vaccines, while poor nations struggle to get enough.
2020-12-15.abstract: As a growing number of vaccines advance through clinical trials, wealthy countries are fueling an extraordinary gap in access around the world, laying claim to more than half the doses that could come on the market by the end of next year.

2020-12-15.title: Court Finds Evidence of Crimes Against Humanity in the Philippines
2020-12-15.abstract: The International Criminal Court has released its latest report on President Rodrigo Duterte’s bloody war on drugs.

2020-12-16.title: Mexico, in Rebuke to U.S., Adopts Measure Restricting Foreign Agents
2020-12-16.abstract: The legislation, which is likely to limit cooperation with U.S. drug agents, was approved after weeks of tension over the Americans’ short-lived arrest of a former Mexican official.

2020-12-16.title: The U.S. and Pfizer are negotiating a deal for more vaccine doses next year.
2020-12-16.abstract: The Trump administration is negotiating a deal to use its power to free up supplies of raw materials to help Pfizer produce tens of millions of additional doses of its Covid-19 vaccine for Americans in the first half of next year, people familiar with the situation said.

2020-12-16.title: A man who rode a Jet Ski across the Irish Sea to see his girlfriend was jailed for defying virus restrictions.
2020-12-16.abstract: Last Thursday, Dale Mclaughlan bought a Jet Ski.

2020-12-16.title: Tom Cruise erupts at the ‘Mission: Impossible’ crew over a breach of Covid-19 protocols.
2020-12-16.abstract: The actor Tom Cruise recently erupted at crew members on the set of “Mission: Impossible 7” over a breach of Covid-19 protocols. His expletive-laden rant was an apparent effort to prevent further disruptions to a film whose production has already been delayed by the pandemic.

2020-12-16.title: Suspect Is Charged With Murder Over Japan Animation Studio Fire
2020-12-16.abstract: Prosecutors accused Shinji Aoba in connection with a blaze in Kyoto last year that killed 36, making it the country’s deadliest attack in decades.

2020-12-17.title: Who Gets to Breathe Clean Air in New Delhi?
2020-12-17.abstract: We measured how much air pollution two children, Monu and Aamya, breathed for a day to see how much inequality makes a difference.

2020-12-17.title: Waste, Negligence and Cronyism: Inside Britain’s Pandemic Spending
2020-12-17.abstract: In the desperate scramble for protective gear and other equipment, political insiders reaped billions.

2020-12-17.title: ‘Nightmare’ Australia Housing Lockdown Called Breach of Human Rights
2020-12-17.abstract: An ombudsman’s report condemned a rushed lockdown of nine public housing towers in Melbourne that left thousands of residents without adequate food and medication and access to fresh air.

2020-12-17.title: M.T.A. officials breathe a sigh of relief with Biden.
2020-12-17.abstract: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority has warned for months that the agency will be forced to make draconian cuts, including slashing New York City’s subway service 40 percent, as the pandemic plunged the nation’s largest public transit agency into its worst financial crisis.

2020-12-17.title: Cold storage, crucial to the vaccine, has caught Wall Street’s eye.
2020-12-17.abstract: As countries prepare to distribute hundreds of millions of Covid-19 vaccines — some of which require storage as cold as the South Pole in winter — the highly specialized operations of companies like PCI Pharma Services, which specializes in packaging and shipping drugs around the world, are in heavy demand. And Wall Street, which likes nothing better than a hot trade with the potential for big profits, is rushing to grab a piece of the action.

2020-12-18.title: International students in Australia prepare to spend the holidays away from family
2020-12-18.abstract: It’s been a rough year surviving the pandemic away from home.

2020-12-18.title: ‘A Social Species’: How Kangaroos Communicate With People
2020-12-18.abstract: Researchers say that kangaroos are the first wild animals to exhibit interspecies communication that is more commonly seen in animals that have evolved alongside humans.

2020-12-18.title: Evictions return, and a sheriff in Massachusetts hunts for a humane approach.
2020-12-18.abstract: With a federal moratorium set to lapse on Dec. 31, America’s vast eviction machine is gradually coming back online, allowing landlords to get rid of nonpaying tenants.

2020-12-18.title: California counts the dead by the hour as its I.C.U.s fill up.
2020-12-18.abstract: Just two weeks ago, Gov. Gavin Newsom warned Californians that the state’s intensive care beds might be full before Christmas.

2020-12-18.title: Masks may soon come with labels saying how well they work.
2020-12-18.abstract: They come in an astonishing variety of colors, designs and materials, but what the 100,000-plus face masks that have hit the market don’t come with is a label that says how well they block infectious particles.

2020-12-19.title: The F.D.A. approves Moderna’s Covid vaccine, adding millions more doses to the U.S. supply.
2020-12-19.abstract: The Food and Drug Administration authorized a second coronavirus vaccine for emergency use, clearing the way for millions more Americans to be immunized next week.

2020-12-19.title: Frontline workers were left off the vaccine list at Stanford Medical Center in Palo Alto. They fought back.
2020-12-19.abstract: This week, some 5,000 doses of Pfizer’s newly authorized coronavirus vaccine arrived on the grounds of Stanford Medical Center in Palo Alto, Calif. — a windfall for frontline health workers eager to receive their first dose of the lifesaving injection.

2020-12-19.title: A Chinese official confirms that a million citizens received unproven homegrown vaccines.
2020-12-19.abstract: More than a million doses of untested Covid-19 vaccines have so far been administered in China to people most at risk of contracting the coronavirus, officials said on Saturday, weeks before a travel rush begins ahead of the Lunar New Year holiday.

2020-12-19.title: ‘R’ is for Rohingya: Sesame Street Creates New Muppets for Refugees
2020-12-19.abstract: Noor and Aziz are Rohingya Muppets who will feature in educational programming that will be shown in refugee camps.

2020-12-19.title: A Covid-19 Surge and Conspiracy Theories Roil Pakistan
2020-12-19.abstract: Public apathy and suspicion, and the government’s inability to enforce health restrictions, are feeding a second wave in a country with a limited social safety net.

2020-12-20.title: A Minnesota state senator, one of several who contracted the coronavirus after local Republican meetings, has died.
2020-12-20.abstract: A Republican state senator from Minnesota who tested positive for the coronavirus last month has died, his wife said in a written statement.

2020-12-20.title: As vaccines arrive, the virus continues to ravage the U.S. and Europe.
2020-12-20.abstract: As the coronavirus continues its surge across the United States and Europe, where vaccinations recently began, total infections around the world have now topped 75 million.

2020-12-20.title: Netanyahu becomes the first Israeli to be inoculated against Covid-19.
2020-12-20.abstract: Arriving at the hospital wearing a short-sleeved polo shirt under his jacket, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu became the first Israeli to be inoculated against Covid-19 on Saturday night, saying he wanted to set an example and encourage all Israelis to get the vaccine.

2020-12-20.title: A Company Made P.P.E. for the World. Now Its Workers Have the Virus.
2020-12-20.abstract: Top Glove, the world’s largest rubber glove maker, has enjoyed record profits in the pandemic, even as thousands of its low-paid workers in Malaysia suffer from a large outbreak of Covid-19.

2020-12-20.title: Nepal Falls Into Political Turmoil. China and India Are Watching.
2020-12-20.abstract: The prime minister dissolved the lower house of Parliament, throwing into doubt the political fortunes of the Himalayan country, which has long swung between Beijing and New Delhi.

2020-12-21.title: She Called Police Over a Neo-Nazi Threat. But the Neo-Nazis Were Inside the Police.
2020-12-21.abstract: Death threats linked to police computers and the discovery of far-right chat groups in police departments across Germany have fed concerns about far-right infiltration.

2020-12-21.title: E.U. Approves Pfizer Vaccine, Setting Stage for High-Stakes Rollout
2020-12-21.abstract: In a landmark collective undertaking, the bloc is poised to start distributing shots to all 27 member nations and their 410 million citizens.

2020-12-21.title: After striking a stimulus deal, Congress faces yet another midnight deadline to approve it.
2020-12-21.abstract: After congressional leaders struck a long-sought agreement on a $900 billion pandemic relief package, lawmakers in both chambers on Monday will race to finalize legislative text and send the measure to President Trump’s desk before government funding lapses.

2020-12-21.title: Moderna’s vaccine rolls out in the U.S.
2020-12-21.abstract: A week after planes and trucks carried the first Covid-19 vaccine across the United States, inching a weary public toward hope, the second was shipped to sites around the country. The Moderna vaccine, like Pfizer’s, requires two shots, spaced weeks apart. The Food and Drug Administration authorized its emergency use on Friday.

2020-12-21.title: China Long Avoided Discussing Mental Health. The Pandemic Changed That.
2020-12-21.abstract: The government has deployed extensive resources to address the spike in depression and anxiety. But social stigmas and long-term challenges remain.

2020-12-22.title: California builds field hospitals and considers rationing care as virus spreads unabated.
2020-12-22.abstract: LOS ANGELES — As California’s new coronavirus cases, hospitalizations and deaths continue to soar, the nation’s most populous state has emerged as the epicenter of the pandemic heading into a dangerous holiday week.

2020-12-22.title: A Hong Kong Activist, Nathan Law, Seeks Political Asylum in Britain
2020-12-22.abstract: The prominent dissident fled to London shortly before a security law was imposed in Hong Kong. Affording him asylum would likely incense China.

2020-12-22.title: Number of Journalists Killed for Their Reporting Doubled in 2020
2020-12-22.abstract: At least 30 journalists were killed this year, according to the Committee to Protect Journalists, with 21 slain as a direct result of their work.

2020-12-22.title: After Bars and Beaches, Gibbons Await a New Home in Thailand
2020-12-22.abstract: The small apes were once widespread across much of Asia. But rampant deforestation and ruthless hunting of the acrobatic animal has greatly reduced its numbers and its habitat.

2020-12-22.title: For Europe, It’s Wave After Wave
2020-12-22.abstract: In a roller coaster year of pandemic, the one constant has been the strain on frontline workers, who are already girding themselves for the next surge.

2020-12-23.title: Ezra F. Vogel, Eminent Scholar of China and Japan, Dies at 90
2020-12-23.abstract: A longtime scholar at Harvard, Professor Vogel wrote books that helped shape how the world viewed the two ascendant economic powers.

2020-12-23.title: Birx says she will retire after Biden transition.
2020-12-23.abstract: Dr. Deborah L. Birx, the White House’s coronavirus response coordinator, said in an interview on Tuesday with the media outlet Newsy that she planned to retire after concluding her role helping the federal government transition to the Biden administration.

2020-12-23.title: Trump demands changes to coronavirus relief bill, calling it a ‘disgrace.’
2020-12-23.abstract: President Trump threatened on Tuesday to derail months of bipartisan work in Congress to deliver $900 billion in pandemic relief, demanding checks to Americans that are more than three times larger than those in the bill, which he called a “disgrace.”

2020-12-23.title: Four Charged Over Blaze That Devastated Australia’s Fraser Island
2020-12-23.abstract: The police said the men started an illegal campfire, but experts said the region would remain vulnerable to more fires as climate change worsens dry conditions.

2020-12-23.title: Xin Xing, Giant Panda With Progeny Around the Globe, Dies at 38
2020-12-23.abstract: The star of China’s Chongqing Zoo was the world’s oldest panda in captivity. More than 150 of her descendants have lived in several countries.

2020-12-24.title: Britain and E.U. Scramble to Reach Brexit Deal Before Christmas
2020-12-24.abstract: With a deadline closing in fast, negotiators were racing to strike a deal, with an agreement said to be imminent.

2020-12-24.title: Additional modeling shows the British variant’s high transmissibility.
2020-12-24.abstract: A team of British scientists released a worrying study on Wednesday of the newly discovered coronavirus variant sweeping Britain. They warned that the variant was so contagious that new control measures, such as closing down schools and universities, might be necessary. Dr. Nicholas Davies, the lead author of the study, said that the findings “are pretty convincing that more rapid vaccination is going to be a really important thing for any country that has to deal with this or similar variants.”

2020-12-24.title: A Flying Elephant, a Teacher’s Hugs: 12 Tales of Pandemic Resilience
2020-12-24.abstract: The virus has been crushing spirits around the world, but some people refuse to be bowed. Here are their stories.

2020-12-24.title: A Covid Christmas Comes to Bethlehem
2020-12-24.abstract: Every Christmas, the quiet West Bank town of Bethlehem becomes a bustling hub for pilgrims, tourists and dignitaries. Not this year.

2020-12-24.title: Influencers May Face Fines as China Tackles Obesity and Food Waste
2020-12-24.abstract: According to a new government report, more than half of the country’s adult population is either overweight or obese. Chinese officials have vowed to address the problem with a campaign.

2020-12-25.title: Adapting to a Holiday Season Stripped of Its Essence
2020-12-25.abstract: Pandemic restrictions mean friends and families will not be gathering this year, but Canadians are coming together in other ways.

2020-12-25.title: Stateless, She Became the Face of a Largely Invisible Plight
2020-12-25.abstract: Maha Mamo had no citizenship. Born in Lebanon of Syrian parents, she was ineligible for either nationality. It took her years of perseverance and some lucky breaks to find a homeland — in Brazil.

2020-12-25.title: U.K. Increasingly Isolated as U.S. Restricts Travel
2020-12-25.abstract: America’s requirement that passengers coming from Britain provide negative coronavirus tests is just the latest woe to strike the country this Christmas period.

2020-12-25.title: Boris Johnson Delivered on Brexit. It May Not Soothe His Unruly Party.
2020-12-25.abstract: Conservatives are united for now over the British prime minister’s trade deal, but as details emerge, he could face challenges from hard-liners and business groups.

2020-12-25.title: Pope Francis Makes Christmas Appeal for Nations to Share Vaccines
2020-12-25.abstract: The pope made his remarks from inside the Vatican instead of before large crowds on St. Peter’s Square, as Christians around the world scaled back or reimagined Christmas traditions.

2020-12-26.title: She Chronicled China’s Crisis. Now She Is Accused of Spreading Lies.
2020-12-26.abstract: Zhang Zhan, who reported about the coronavirus from Wuhan during the lockdown, will face trial next week, in the first known case against a citizen journalist from the crisis.

2020-12-26.title: France and Japan report cases of the virus variant that prompted lockdowns in Britain.
2020-12-26.abstract: France and Japan have reported their first cases of the coronavirus variant that prompted lockdowns in Britain and is feared to be more contagious than other strains.

2020-12-26.title: As Virus Resurges in Africa, Doctors Fear the Worst Is Yet to Come
2020-12-26.abstract: The coronavirus killed far fewer people in Africa than in Europe and the Americas, leading to a widespread perception that it was a disease of the West. Now, a tide of new cases on the continent is raising alarms.

2020-12-26.title: 1895: Wretched Weather Mars Christmas in London
2020-12-26.abstract: Rain, snow and driving sleet hit the British capital 125 years ago, though a program offering poor children a hamper of holiday foods was a bright spot.

2020-12-26.title: The Brexit Fight, Through a Reporter’s Prism of a Changed Continent
2020-12-26.abstract: Alan Cowell, a longtime New York Times correspondent, recalls a different Europe, one of currency controls, cumbersome paperwork and burdensome cross-border regulations.

2020-12-27.title: Welcoming a New Year at an Ancient Festival in Pakistan
2020-12-27.abstract: Chawmos, a festival of Pakistan’s tiny Kalash community, is a portrait in contrasts: solemn ritual and joyous dancing, gender segregation and public flirtation, togetherness and isolation.

2020-12-27.title: E.U.’s Mass Vaccination Campaign Starts, With Nursing Homes as Focus
2020-12-27.abstract: An ambitious plan to inoculate more than 450 million people comes at a precarious moment for the continent, with many countries struggling with their worst outbreaks since the start of the pandemic.

2020-12-27.title: In efforts to vaccinate Europeans, a push to overcome skepticism.
2020-12-27.abstract: Prime Minister Andrej Babis of the Czech Republic received his country’s first coronavirus vaccination on Sunday, followed by a World War II veteran, as the nation began both a vaccine campaign and a stricter lockdown to confront a second wave of infection that is sweeping thorough Eastern Europe.

2020-12-27.title: In a Village of Widows, the Opium Trade Has Taken a Deadly Toll
2020-12-27.abstract: Afghan men in an impoverished border settlement die trying to smuggle opium into Iran, leaving behind loved ones forced to survive on their own.

2020-12-27.title: Ex-Military Officers Criticize Spain’s Government and Praise Franco
2020-12-27.abstract: The comments by former officers, and some current ones, struck a raw nerve in a country that had been ruled by a military dictatorship.

2020-12-28.title: Italy Turns to Flower Power to Help Spread Vaccine Message
2020-12-28.abstract: The architect Stefano Boeri has designed primrose-themed pavilions where coronavirus shots will be given, but not everybody is thrilled with the idea.

2020-12-28.title: Chinese Citizen Journalist Sentenced to 4 Years for Covid Reporting
2020-12-28.abstract: Zhang Zhan, a former lawyer, is the first known person to be tried for challenging the Chinese government’s narrative about the coronavirus pandemic.

2020-12-28.title: Refugees Come Under Fire as Old Foes Fight in Concert in Ethiopia
2020-12-28.abstract: Forces from neighboring Eritrea have joined the war in northern Ethiopia, and have rampaged through refugee camps committing human rights violations, officials and witnesses say.

2020-12-28.title: Italy uses flowers to help spread a vaccine message.
2020-12-28.abstract: This has been an ugly year for Italy.

2020-12-28.title: Saudi Activist Who Fought for Women’s Right to Drive Is Sentenced to Prison
2020-12-28.abstract: Loujain al-Hathloul, who was arrested just before the driving ban was lifted, was charged with undermining the kingdom in a case that supporters decried as a political persecution.

2020-12-29.title: A Far-Right Terrorism Suspect With a Refugee Disguise: The Tale of Franco A.
2020-12-29.abstract: A German officer is facing trial on terrorism charges. At a volatile time for Western democracy, his story mirrors the story of Germany itself.

2020-12-29.title: Remote schooling brings bigger losses for children still learning to speak English.
2020-12-29.abstract: While the disruptions of 2020 have threatened learning loss for nearly all students across the country, the toll has been especially severe for students from immigrant homes where English is rarely if ever spoken.

2020-12-29.title: Employers are struggling to manage unused vacation days.
2020-12-29.abstract: In a typical year, New York employees of the magazine publisher Condé Nast must use their vacation days before late December or lose them — a common policy across corporate America.

2020-12-29.title: South Africa will make plenty of vaccine. But its citizens are at the back of the line.
2020-12-29.abstract: Over the past few months, rich nations like the United States and Britain have cut deals with multiple drug manufacturers and secured enough doses of coronavirus vaccine to inoculate their citizens many times over. China and Russia have conducted their own trials and begun mass vaccination programs.

2020-12-29.title: The retail industry’s tumultuous year began before the pandemic.
2020-12-29.abstract: The retail industry was in the midst of a transformation before 2020. But the pandemic accelerated that change, fundamentally reordering how and where people shop, and rippling across the broader economy.

2020-12-30.title: Hong Kong Protesters Who Fled by Boat Are Sentenced to Prison in China
2020-12-30.abstract: The pro-democracy activists, who hoped to reach Taiwan, were caught by the Chinese Coast Guard and tried in the mainland’s opaque criminal justice system.

2020-12-30.title: A Chinese coronavirus vaccine has proved effective, its maker said.
2020-12-30.abstract: A Chinese pharmaceutical company on Wednesday said late-stage drug trials showed that one of its coronavirus vaccines was effective, delivering positive results that could pave the way for the global rollout of hundreds of millions of Chinese vaccine doses in the coming months.

2020-12-30.title: U.K. Authorizes Covid-19 Vaccine From Oxford and AstraZeneca
2020-12-30.abstract: Health officials hope to soon vaccinate up to two million people per week as the country’s hospitals are overwhelmed by cases of a new, more contagious coronavirus variant.

2020-12-30.title: Argentina Legalizes Abortion, a Milestone in a Conservative Region
2020-12-30.abstract: The Senate vote on Wednesday was a major victory for Latin America’s growing feminist movement, and its ripple effects are likely to be widespread.

2020-12-30.title: Jonathan Pollard, Spy for Israel, Gets Hero’s Welcome From Netanyahu: ‘You’re Home’
2020-12-30.abstract: Mr. Pollard, who gave a range of classified documents to Israel starting in 1984, recently completed his parole. His case was a longstanding irritant to American-Israeli ties.

2020-12-31.title: Ohio offered nursing home workers early dibs on a vaccine. Many said no.
2020-12-31.abstract: Although the vast majority of nursing home residents in Ohio are accepting a vaccine for Covid-19, the same cannot be said for the staff in these facilities, Gov. Mike DeWine said on Wednesday.

2020-12-31.title: Jobs, Houses and Cows: China’s Costly Drive to Erase Extreme Poverty
2020-12-31.abstract: China has spent heavily to help its poorest citizens, an approach that few developing countries can afford and even Beijing may struggle to sustain.

2020-12-31.title: Joy survives: Readers share memories of the good moments from this year.
2020-12-31.abstract: Even in this dark year, there have been moments of lightness, growth and utter joy.

2020-12-31.title: Jimmy Lai, Hong Kong Pro-Democracy Mogul, Is Ordered Back to Jail
2020-12-31.abstract: Mr. Lai, the most prominent figure charged under Hong Kong’s sweeping national security law, had been released on bail just days before Thursday’s hearing.

2020-12-31.title: India imposes a curfew in New Delhi to curb New Year celebrations.
2020-12-31.abstract: The authorities in India are imposing a night curfew in the capital, New Delhi, restricting celebrations in a metropolis known for hosting grand events on New Year’s Eve after more cases of a new variant of coronavirus were reported across the country.
